food_pairing.name: Barolo with brasato al Barolo
food_pairing.pairing: Barolo DOCG with brasato al Barolo, beef braised in the same wine
food_pairing.description: Piedmont's defining wine-and-meat marriage: beef slow-braised in Nebbiolo, paired back with a structured Barolo whose firm tannins cut the richness and whose tar and dried-cherry notes echo the braise.

food_pairing.name: Barolo with tajarin and white truffle
food_pairing.pairing: Barolo DOCG with tajarin, thin egg pasta, and shaved Alba white truffle
food_pairing.description: The Langhe autumn ritual: hand-cut tajarin under shavings of Tuber magnatum from Alba, with an aged Nebbiolo whose savoury, earthy core resonates with the truffle and whose acidity lifts the egg-rich pasta.

food_pairing.name: Barbera with agnolotti del plin
food_pairing.pairing: Barbera d'Asti DOCG with agnolotti del plin, pinched meat-stuffed pasta
food_pairing.description: The Monferrato everyday pairing: tiny hand-pinched agnolotti in butter or jus, with Barbera's bright, racy acidity cutting the rich meat filling and its fruit refreshing the palate.

food_pairing.name: Barbera with bollito misto
food_pairing.pairing: Barbera d'Alba DOC with bollito misto, Piedmontese mixed boiled meats
food_pairing.description: The Sunday classic of the Langhe: a steaming platter of boiled meats with salsa verde and mostarda, lifted by Barbera's high acidity which slices through the fats and refreshes between bites.

food_pairing.name: Moscato d'Asti with torta di nocciole
food_pairing.pairing: Moscato d'Asti DOCG with torta di nocciole, Piedmont hazelnut cake
food_pairing.description: The region's dessert pairing: a moist Piedmont Hazelnut cake with the lightly sparkling, low-alcohol Moscato d'Asti, whose peach, apricot and rose petal lift the nutty crumb without cloying.

food_pairing.name: Gavi with seafood fritto misto
food_pairing.pairing: Gavi DOCG with fritto misto di mare, mixed fried seafood
food_pairing.description: Although landlocked, Gavi's racy Cortese is Piedmont's seafood white: its mineral freshness and citrus cut through a platter of crisp fried seafood and frame raw shellfish.

food_pairing.name: Roero Arneis with vitello tonnato
food_pairing.pairing: Roero Arneis DOCG with vitello tonnato, cold sliced veal in tuna sauce
food_pairing.description: Piedmont's signature antipasto meets its signature white: thin-sliced cold veal under a creamy tuna-and-caper sauce, with floral, almond-edged Arneis whose fresh acidity balances the richness.

food_pairing.name: Langhe Riesling with saffron risotto
food_pairing.pairing: Langhe Riesling DOC with saffron risotto in the Milanese style
food_pairing.description: A cross-border pairing reaching toward neighbouring Lombardy: Piedmont's rare high-altitude dry Riesling, all mineral cut and racy acidity, frames the buttery saffron richness of a Milanese risotto.
